title: Baton Contract Registry
description: Release-level registry of cross-slice wire contracts (planner-emitted, sibling to board.json at docs/release/<name>/contracts.json). The touchpoint matrix proves file-level disjointness between tracks; this record proves contract-level coherence between slices — every agreement two specs share (an endpoint's body shape, a header, an env var, a schema version) gets exactly one owning slice, a pinning live test when consumed, and an explicit edge-config disposition. Closes the 2026-07-10 failure class where every build halt lived on an EDGE between two specs that no single spec owned. ADVISORY while the grading gate (reference implementation: sworn lint contracts) is unshipped: planners emit it, nothing hard-fails on absence; it flips to required when the gate ships (baton#59 skew-window policy).

id_token.description: A clean identifier token. Disallows whitespace and ':' so a newline-fusion defect fails validation by construction.

version.description: Artefact schema version for forward compatibility.

release.description: Release identifier this registry belongs to, e.g. 2026-07-10-one-current-position.

kind.description: The seam class. Each kind names a contract with a DISTINCT config/middleware surface the linter treats specially — that is the bar for adding a kind. http-endpoint: method+path; its shape subsumes query params, content types, status codes, and error envelopes (no separate kinds for those). header: a request/response header crossing middleware (e.g. If-Match). cookie: name+attributes crossing cookie policy and CORS credentials mode. env-var: configuration one slice defines, another reads. edge-config: a middleware/config surface itself (CORS allowlist, proxy rewrite, body-size limit, rate limiter) — the sibling entry that http-endpoint/header/cookie contracts must reference via edge_config. schema-version: a versioned payload/document schema shared across slices. db-schema: a table/column/index contract between a migration slice and its readers. storage-key: a localStorage/KV/cache key + value shape. feature-flag: a flag one slice defines and others branch on. auth-scope: a role/scope/entitlement name and where it is enforced. event: a named event/message and payload shape over any bus (SSE, WS, queue). Client-side SPA routes are deliberately NOT a kind: a broken internal link crosses no middleware surface and is owned by the Rule 10 journey walk.

shape.description: The agreement itself: body/payload shape, allowed values, status codes, attributes. Precise enough that a consumer spec author cannot re-derive it wrongly.

owner.description: Exactly one owning slice id. The owner's spec carries the AC that binds the live test; the linter fails on two owners for one surface.

consumers.description: Slice ids that depend on this contract without owning it.

live_test.description: Reference to the owner's live (unmocked, real-transport) test that pins this contract, resolvable by the same logic as trace-gate test_refs. REQUIRED when consumers is non-empty: a consumed contract with no live pin is exactly the shared-blind-spot seam (mock and spec encoding the same wrong assumption).

fixtures.description: Path to the request/response fixture file recorded by the owner's passing live test (mock parity, Rule 10 sub-rule). Consumer mocks at this boundary must load these fixtures, never hand-written shapes. Optional while the mock-parity gate is unshipped.

edge_config.description: Disposition of the middleware/config surfaces between the two ends: either the id of the sibling edge-config contract entry that owns them (e.g. 'C-04'), or an explicit waiver 'n/a: <reason>' (e.g. 'n/a: same-origin, no preflight'). REQUIRED for http-endpoint, header, and cookie kinds — this is the catcher for the class where a header is specced on both ends while the CORS allowlist between them is owned by nobody.

rationale.description: Optional prose: why this contract exists, trade-offs, provenance.
